Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Imerco Searches. 29 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Imerco search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas

A sample of the 60 real customer queries we tested

påske tilbud fiskars skærebræt le creuset støbejern gryde billige gryder kaffekande gryde sæt begynder køkken le cruset gryde køkkengrej stor wok pande pander eva solo kedel grydeskåle stegepander køkkenting le creuset gryde æbleskivepander tilbud zwiling knive gryde køkken grej scand gryd orrefors salt & peber
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Imerco's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 29 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Add an intent & boost layer before keyword ranking (brand, product-type, price, and bestseller signals) — This addresses the core ranking failures seen in Product Discovery, Typo Tolerance, Brand Search, and Shopping Context where higher-scoring results are consistently buried below lower-scoring ones.

48%
revenue risk. 29 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
29
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
27
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
4
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
70%
Right products found, ranked too low
#5
Avg. best result position
53%
Best result outside top 3
16%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder" gets zero results — a blank page — even though you carry it (e.g. "Casa Kids Begynderkniv" shows up when they instead search "begynder køkken").
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “scanpan stegepande” — The highest relevance result 'STS Stegepandesæt - 2 dele' (0.582) was ranked #8 instead of #1.
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.
Product Discovery
Broad category
Collection routing
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “madlavning” — The query 'madlavning' (cooking) returned only airfryer accessories and small kitchen items, missing core cooking categories.
Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “zwiling knive” — The query 'zwiling knive' contains a typo ('zwiling' instead of 'Zwilling'), but the search engine failed to correct it.
Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.
Typo Tolerance
Merged words
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “pandekagepander” — The highest relevance result 'Valnød Pandekagepande' (0.812) was buried at position #13, while lower-scoring results like 'Crepepande' (0.451) and…
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.
Typo Tolerance
Split word
Pattern tuning

Example “stege pander” — The search engine correctly retrieved relevant products for 'stege pander' (split compound word for 'stegepande'), but ranked them poorly.
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.

Special character
Pattern tuning

Example “æbleskivepander” — The highest relevance result 'Emaljeret Æbleskivepande' (0.844) was buried at position #9, while lower-scoring results like 'Æbleskivebager' (0.578) appeared…

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 3 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “scand” — The search correctly retrieved relevant products matching 'scand' prefix (Scandic, Scandia, Scandinavia brands), but ranked them terribly.
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.
Language Understanding
Synonym
Vocabulary mapping
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “madlavningsudstyr” — The search returned zero results for the Danish query 'madlavningsudstyr' (cooking equipment), despite the analysis indicating that relevant products exist…
Does search connect customer vocabulary to the store's preferred product wording?
Next tuning move: Expand synonym dictionaries and semantic embeddings for the product language customers actually use.
Language Understanding
Natural language
Intent translation
3 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed” — The search engine returned zero results for the Danish query 'jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed' (I need a good pot for my new apartment).
Can longer, conversational needs be translated into product intent?
Next tuning move: Extract intent phrases from longer queries before ranking products.
Brand & Model Search
Brand search
Brand protection

Example “fiskars” — The highest relevance Fiskars product 'Essential Y-formet Skrællekniv' (0.428 relevance) was buried at position #12, while much lower relevance products…
Are brand requests recognized and protected from unrelated results?
Next tuning move: Detect brand entities and boost or filter to products from the requested brand.
Shopping Context
Plural / singular
Term normalization

At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.

Does search normalize simple wording variants such as singular and plural terms?
Next tuning move: Normalize singular, plural, and close inflection variants before retrieval.
Shopping Context
Seasonal / occasion
Occasion matching

Example “sommer grillpander” — The search returned zero results for 'sommer grillpander' despite this being a reasonable query for summer grill pans.
Can seasonal or occasion-based needs map to relevant categories and products?
Next tuning move: Add occasion and season tags, then use them as ranking signals for broad needs.

Shopping Context
Subjective attribute
Merchandising language

Example “billige gryder” — The search returned zero results for the query 'billige gryder' (cheap pots in Danish).
Can descriptive shopping language like premium, casual, or comfortable be mapped sensibly?
Next tuning move: Connect subjective descriptors to curated tags, product copy, and merchandising attributes.
Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 3 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “stor wok pande” — Query 'stor wok pande' returned zero results despite containing recognizable product terms.
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

40%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 40% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 39%.

Evidence

  • "scanpan stegepande": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "eva solo kedel": 0/1 relevant in top 5
  • "royal copenhagen": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "madlavning": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

68%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 41 of 60 queries (68%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "madlavningsudstyr": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "sommer grillpander": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

4
queries with attribute loss

Result

4 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "stor wok pande": dropped stor, wok, pande; partially preserved none
  • "rustfrit stål køkkenredskaber": dropped rustfrit, kkenredskaber; partially preserved st
  • "le creuset støbejern gryde": dropped le, creuset, st, bejern; partially preserved none
  • "scanpan non-stick stegepande": dropped scanpan, non, stick; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

1
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "skærebræt": 1 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "skærebræt": 1 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "stegepande": 2 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "køkkenredskab": 5 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "gryder og stegepander": 6 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Add an intent & boost layer before keyword ranking (brand, product-type, price, and bestseller signals)

This addresses the core ranking failures seen in Product Discovery, Typo Tolerance, Brand Search, and Shopping Context where higher-scoring results are consistently buried below lower-scoring ones.

Critical Quick Win
2

Add Danish language synonym mapping and translations

This fixes Language Understanding failures for terms like "madlavningsudstyr", "kaffekande", and natural language queries, plus Shopping Context issues with seasonal terms.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Implement fuzzy matching for brand names and common misspellings

This resolves Typo Tolerance failures where misspelled brands like "zwiling" (Zwilling) return irrelevant results instead of the intended brand products.

Critical Medium Effort
4

Configure brand constraint filtering

This fixes Product Discovery issues where brand-specific searches return wrong brands (like "eva solo kedel" returning Nordic Kitchen products).

Critical Quick Win
5

Add category mapping for broad Danish cooking terms

This addresses Product Discovery failures where "madlavning" returns only airfryer accessories instead of core cooking equipment categories.

Critical Medium Effort
6

Implement Danish natural language query processing

This fixes Language Understanding failures for conversational queries like "jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed" that currently return zero results.

Critical Major Project
7

Add support for Danish price and size qualifiers

This resolves Filters & Constraints failures where terms like "billige" (cheap) and "stor" (large) prevent any results from appearing.

Critical Medium Effort
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 47% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #5 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 16% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 madlavningsudstyr Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the Danish query 'madlavningsudstyr' (cooking equipment), despite the analysis indicating that relevant products exist (best result was at position #16, suggesting products are in the catalog but not being retrieved)

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#2 jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search engine returned zero results for the Danish query 'jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed' (I need a good pot for my new apartment). Despite the rationale indicating this tests a real pot category, no products were retrieved at all.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#3 kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search engine returned zero results for the Danish query 'kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder' (can you recommend some kitchen equipment for a beginner). Despite the query clearly asking for kitchen equipment recommendations for beginners, no products were retrieved at all.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#4 sommer grillpander Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for 'sommer grillpander' despite this being a reasonable query for summer grill pans. The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the best result was displaced to position 16, indicating the engine found nothing relevant in the top positions.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#5 nytår gave køkken Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the Danish query 'nytår gave køkken' (New Year gift kitchen), despite this being a reasonable seasonal search that should surface kitchen-related gift items suitable for New Year occasions.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#6 gave til køkkenchef Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the query 'gave til køkkenchef' (gift for chef in Danish). Despite the site having kitchen-related categories that should contain chef gifts, the engine failed to return any products at all.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"le creuset gryde"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #8

The two highest-scoring results 'Le Creuset Gryde' (relevance: 0.922) were buried at positions #8 and #12, while lower-scoring 'Le Creuset Signature Gryde' products (relevance: 0.918) dominated positions #1, #2, #5, #7, #9-#11, #13-#14. The best matching results were displaced by 7 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Le Creuset Signature Gryde
  2. Le Creuset Signature Gryde
  3. Le Creuset Signature Rund Gryde
  4. Le Creuset Minigryde
  5. Le Creuset Signature Gryde

Should Have Seen

  1. Le Creuset Gryde
  2. Le Creuset Gryde
  3. Le Creuset Signature Gryde

Best match was buried at position #8.

"scanpan stegepande"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #8

The highest relevance result 'STS Stegepandesæt - 2 dele' (0.582) was ranked #8 instead of #1. The top 3 positions were occupied by much lower relevance results: 'Classic Induction Stegepande' (0.477), 'IQ Stegepande' (0.467), and 'Impact Stegepande' (0.457). All results are Scanpan frying pans as requested, but the ranking algorithm is not properly ordering by relevance scores.

Customer Saw

  1. Classic Induction Stegepande
  2. IQ Stegepande
  3. Impact Stegepande
  4. Impact Stegepande
  5. STS Stegepande

Should Have Seen

  1. STS Stegepandesæt - 2 dele
  2. STS Stegepande
  3. STS Stegepande

Best match was buried at position #8.

"madlavning"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #2

The query 'madlavning' (cooking) returned only airfryer accessories and small kitchen items, missing core cooking categories. Results are dominated by 'Madtang til airfryer' (#1), 'Madspyd til airfryer' (#2), 'Oliesprayflaske til airfryer' (#3), and multiple 'Form til airfryer' entries. The engine appears to be matching 'mad' (food) as a keyword in product titles rather than understanding 'madlavning' as a broad cooking category that should include gryder (pots), stegepander (pans), and køkkenredskaber (kitchen tools).

Customer Saw

  1. Madtang til airfryer
  2. Madspyd til airfryer - 6 stk.
  3. Oliesprayflaske til airfryer
  4. Brødmåtte
  5. Æggeholder

Should Have Seen

  1. Madspyd til airfryer - 6 stk.
  2. Madtang til airfryer
  3. Muffinform til airfryer - 6 stk.
"eva solo kedel"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #1

Query 'eva solo kedel' specifically requested Eva Solo brand kettles, but the only result returned was 'Nordic Kitchen Induktionskedel' - a completely different brand (Nordic Kitchen instead of Eva Solo). The search engine ignored the brand constraint entirely.

Customer Saw

  1. Nordic Kitchen Induktionskedel (594,95)

Should Have Seen

  1. Nordic Kitchen Induktionskedel (594,95)
"tilbud"
Broad category · Moderate · Best result at position #1

The search correctly identified relevant products for 'tilbud' (offers/sales) but ranked them poorly. High-relevance items like 'Canteen Opbevaringssæt - 20 stk.' (0.396 relevance) appeared at position #8, 'Hvid Riflet Krus - 2 stk.' (0.369 relevance) was buried at position #15, and 'Stainless Steel Stegepande' (0.307 relevance) was pushed down to position #12. Meanwhile, lower-relevance items like 'Hvid Riflet Middagstallerken' (0.277 relevance) and 'Hvid Riflet Frokosttallerken' (0.275 relevance) occupied positions #2 and #4.

Customer Saw

  1. Blå Kagetallerken - Online særtilbud
  2. Hvid Riflet Middagstallerken
  3. Blå Mega Riflet Krus - 2 stk.
  4. Hvid Riflet Frokosttallerken
  5. Legio Nova Serveringsfad - 3 stk.

Should Have Seen

  1. Blå Kagetallerken - Online særtilbud
  2. Canteen Opbevaringssæt - 20 stk.
  3. Legio Nova Serveringsfad - 3 stk.

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"pandekagepander"
Merged words · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result 'Valnød Pandekagepande' (0.812) was buried at position #13, while lower-scoring results like 'Crepepande' (0.451) and 'Blinispande' (0.424) appeared in positions #3 and #7. Multiple identical 'Pandekagepande' products with 0.789 relevance were scattered across positions #1, #2, and #15 instead of being grouped at the top.

Customer Saw

  1. Pandekagepande
  2. Pandekagepande
  3. Crepepande
  4. Premium Pandekagepande
  5. Reinvent Pandekagepande

Should Have Seen

  1. Valnød Pandekagepande
  2. Pandekagepande
  3. Pandekagepande

Best match was buried at position #13.

"scand"
Partial query · Critical · Best result at position #13

The search correctly retrieved relevant products matching 'scand' prefix (Scandic, Scandia, Scandinavia brands), but ranked them terribly. The best matches 'Scandic Pendel' (0.566 relevance) were buried at positions #13 and #15, while completely irrelevant airfryer accessories with much lower relevance scores (0.305-0.332) occupied the top 3 positions. High-relevance Scandia products like 'Scandia Bordkniv' (0.559) and 'Scandia Desserttallerken' (0.543) were also pushed down to positions #12 and #5 respectively.

Customer Saw

  1. Silikoneform til airfryer
  2. Papirform til airfryer - 100 stk.
  3. Papirform til airfryer - 300 stk.
  4. Scandinavia Candles Alterlys
  5. Scandia Desserttallerken

Should Have Seen

  1. Scandic Pendel
  2. Scandic Pendel
  3. Scandia Bordkniv

Best match was buried at position #13.

"zwiling knive"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #10

The query 'zwiling knive' contains a typo ('zwiling' instead of 'Zwilling'), but the search engine failed to correct it. The two highest relevance results are 'Twinny Børnekniv' (0.457 relevance) which are actual Zwilling brand products, but they were buried at positions #10 and #11. Instead, the engine returned generic knife-related products like 'Steakknive - 4 dele' (#1), 'Four Star Knivblok' (#2), and 'Knivblok' (#3-4) that have no connection to the Zwilling brand.

Customer Saw

  1. Steakknive - 4 dele
  2. Four Star Knivblok - 7 dele
  3. Knivblok
  4. Knivblok
  5. Knivmagnet

Should Have Seen

  1. Twinny Børnekniv
  2. Twinny Børnekniv
  3. Steakknive - 4 dele

Best match was buried at position #10.

"køkkenudstyr"
Merged words · Critical · Best result at position #10

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant kitchen equipment products but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance product 'Håndmixer' (0.621) was buried at position #10, while the lowest relevance product 'Burgerpresser' (0.486) was ranked #1. Other high-relevance items like 'Skærebræt m. saftrille' (0.609) and 'Pizza- og urteskærer' (0.605) were ranked at positions #7 and #12 respectively, despite being more relevant than the top-ranked results.

Customer Saw

  1. Burgerpresser
  2. Classic Grydeskesæt - 4 dele
  3. Stainless Steel Grydesæt - 3 dele
  4. Canteen Opbevaringssæt - 20 stk.
  5. Stainless Steel Stegepande

Should Have Seen

  1. Håndmixer
  2. Skærebræt m. saftrille
  3. Pizza- og urteskærer

Best match was buried at position #10.

"stege pander"
Split word · Critical · Best result at position #9

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant products for 'stege pander' (split compound word for 'stegepande'), but ranked them poorly. The five highest-relevance results 'Stainless Steel Stegepande' (relevance 0.766) were buried at positions #9, #11, #12, #13, and #15. Meanwhile, lower-relevance results like 'Håndtag til stegepander' (0.719) and 'Emaljeret Stegeplade' (0.531) occupied the top positions. The best matching result was displaced by 8 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Håndtag til stegepander
  2. Emaljeret Stegeplade
  3. Emaljeret Stegeplade
  4. Seasoned Vendbar Stegeplade
  5. Seasoned Vendbar Stegeplade

Should Have Seen

  1. Stainless Steel Stegepande
  2. Stainless Steel Stegepande
  3. Stainless Steel Stegepande

Best match was buried at position #9.

Language Understanding

5 examples
"madlavningsudstyr"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for the Danish query 'madlavningsudstyr' (cooking equipment), despite the analysis indicating that relevant products exist (best result was at position #16, suggesting products are in the catalog but not being retrieved)

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed"
Natural language · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search engine returned zero results for the Danish query 'jeg har brug for en god gryde til min nye lejlighed' (I need a good pot for my new apartment). Despite the rationale indicating this tests a real pot category, no products were retrieved at all.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder"
Natural language · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search engine returned zero results for the Danish query 'kan i anbefale noget køkkenudstyr til en begynder' (can you recommend some kitchen equipment for a beginner). Despite the query clearly asking for kitchen equipment recommendations for beginners, no products were retrieved at all.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"pander"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #11

The three highest-relevance results (Folken Støbejernspande at 0.570, Nanobond Gryde- og pandesæt at 0.570, and another Folken Støbejernspande at 0.570) were buried at positions #11, #14, and #15 respectively. Meanwhile, lower-relevance pancake-specific products dominated the top positions: Pandekagepande (0.391) at #1, Professional Brandepande (0.371) at #2, and another Pandekagepande (0.391) at #3. The best result was displaced by 10 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Pandekagepande
  2. Professional Brandepande
  3. Pandekagepande
  4. Impact Chefpande m. låg
  5. Pandebeskytter - 3 stk.

Should Have Seen

  2. Nanobond Gryde- og pandesæt - 10 dele
  3. Folken Støbejernspande

Best match was buried at position #11.

"køkkenredskab"
Synonym · Moderate · Best result at position #4

The highest relevance result 'Køkkenredskabssæt - 6 dele' (0.871) was ranked at position #4 instead of #1. The search engine correctly retrieved relevant kitchen utensil products but failed to order them by their relevance scores, placing three lower-scoring results (0.863) ahead of the best match.

Customer Saw

  1. Resource Køkkenredskabssæt - 4 dele
  2. Køkkenredskabssæt - 4 dele
  3. Køkkenredskabssæt - 4 dele
  4. Køkkenredskabssæt - 6 dele
  5. Køkkenredskabssæt - 3 dele

  2. Resource Køkkenredskabssæt - 4 dele
  3. Køkkenredskabssæt - 4 dele

Best match was buried at position #4.

"fiskars"
Brand search · Critical · Best result at position #12

The highest relevance Fiskars product 'Essential Y-formet Skrællekniv' (0.428 relevance) was buried at position #12, while much lower relevance products appeared at the top: 'Dåseåbner' (0.287 relevance) at #1, 'Hard Face Stegepande' (0.301 relevance) at #2, and another 'Hard Face Stegepande' (0.301 relevance) at #4. The top 3 positions contained products with relevance scores of 0.287, 0.301, and 0.381, while better matches with scores of 0.428, 0.400, and 0.391 were ranked at positions 12, 13, and 10 respectively.

5 examples
"sommer grillpander"
Seasonal / occasion · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for 'sommer grillpander' despite this being a reasonable query for summer grill pans. The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the best result was displaced to position 16, indicating the engine found nothing relevant in the top positions.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

Seasonal / occasion · Critical · Best result at position #16

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"gave til køkkenchef"
Use case · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for the query 'gave til køkkenchef' (gift for chef in Danish). Despite the site having kitchen-related categories that should contain chef gifts, the engine failed to return any products at all.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"professionel madlavning"
Use case · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for the query 'professionel madlavning' (professional cooking in Danish). Despite this being a clear use case query for professional kitchen equipment, no products were retrieved at all.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"billige gryder"
Subjective attribute · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for the query 'billige gryder' (cheap pots in Danish). The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the displacement metric indicates the best result was at position 16, suggesting there are relevant products in the catalog that weren't retrieved.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

Filters & Constraints

4 examples
"stor wok pande"
Multi-attribute · Critical · Best result at position #16

Query 'stor wok pande' returned zero results despite containing recognizable product terms. The query appears to be Danish/Norwegian for 'large wok pan' but the search engine failed to handle the misspelling or language variation, returning no results at all.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

"scanpan non-stick stegepande"
Multi-attribute · Minor · Best result at position #3

The search correctly retrieved relevant Scanpan non-stick frying pans, but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance products (CS+ Stegepande at 0.477) appeared at positions #3, #5, and #8, while a lower relevance product (Classic Induction Stegepande at 0.424) was ranked #1. All top results are Scanpan brand stegepande (frying pans) matching the query intent.

Customer Saw

  1. Classic Induction Stegepande
  2. IQ Stegepande
  3. CS+ Stegepande
  4. CS+ Rec. Stegepande
  5. CS+ Stegepande

Should Have Seen

  1. CS+ Stegepande
  2. CS+ Stegepande
  3. CS+ Stegepande
"rustfrit stål køkkenredskaber"
Multi-attribute · Minor · Best result at position #2

The search engine retrieved relevant kitchen utensils but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance result 'Smartline Redskabskrukke' (0.447) was placed at #2 instead of #1, while 'Good Grips Redskabsholder' (0.371) was ranked #1. Additionally, 'Profi Plus Kødhammer' with relevance 0.414 was buried at position #15, and 'Helix Kartoffelmoser' with relevance 0.396 was at position #11.

Customer Saw

  1. Good Grips Redskabsholder
  2. Smartline Redskabskrukke
  3. Piskeris - 2 stk.
  4. Piskeris
  5. Pro Piskeris Lille

Should Have Seen

  1. Smartline Redskabskrukke
  2. Piskeris - 2 stk.
  3. Profi Plus Kødhammer
"le creuset støbejern gryde"
Multi-attribute · Minor · Best result at position #1

The search correctly retrieved relevant Le Creuset cast iron pots (gryde), but the ranking is suboptimal. Products with identical relevance scores (0.447) for 'Signature Gryde' are scattered throughout positions #1, #2, #4, #6-#11, and #15, while 'Signature Rund Gryde' with slightly lower scores (0.428) are interspersed at positions #3 and #5. This creates an inconsistent user experience where identical products appear at different ranks.

Customer Saw

  1. Signature Gryde
  2. Signature Gryde
  3. Signature Rund Gryde
  4. Signature Gryde
  5. Signature Rund Gryde

Should Have Seen

  1. Signature Gryde
  2. Signature Gryde
  3. Signature Gryde
Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 28 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 20 queries ("eva solo kedel", "gryder og stegepander", "tilbud"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
